romanized: F&N Bīng Qí Lín Sū Dá

region_of_origin: Singapore. Fraser & Neave (F&N) is Singapore's oldest food and beverage company, established in 1883. F&N Ice Cream Soda is a culturally iconic bright-green carbonated soft drink widely associated with the Singapore ice cream float tradition, sold in 325ml cans and widely available at hawker centres, kopitiams, and supermarkets across Singapore and Malaysia.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general class for carbonated soft drinks (FOODON_03301589). No brand-specific entry exists for F&N Ice Cream Soda; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN000A covers carbonated soft drinks as a category. No brand-specific F&N Ice Cream Soda entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for this branded carbonated soft drink.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is 1 can (325ml / approximately 325g), providing approximately 137 kcal and 34g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: F&N Ice Cream Soda — Nutrition Information Panel, Fraser & Neave Singapore 325ml can product label
nutrient_source.organization: Fraser & Neave Ltd, Singapore — mandatory Singapore Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) per HPB labelling requirements
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the mandatory Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) printed on the F&N Ice Cream Soda 325ml can, as required under Singapore's Health Promotion Board (HPB) food labelling regulations. Values are per 100ml (treated as per 100g given density ~1.0 g/ml for this sugar-water solution). Energy 42 kcal/100g, carbohydrate 10.5g/100g (all sugars), protein 0g, fat 0g confirmed on label. This is an exact product-label match for a Singapore-manufactured branded beverage; the NIP is a regulatory document and constitutes an approved primary source for commercial packaged foods under Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages — analogous estimate based on sucrose and glucose solutions; University of Sydney GI Database reference values for carbonated sugar drinks
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for F&N Ice Cream Soda specifically. GI of 95 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database values for carbonated sugar-sweetened beverages and sucrose solutions, which consistently yield GI values in the range 63–97 depending on sugar concentration and carbonation. At 10.5g sugar per 100ml (all sucrose/glucose), this beverage is functionally equivalent to a concentrated sucrose solution in carbonated water with no dietary fibre or fat to attenuate absorption. GI ~95 is the defensible upper-range estimate consistent with published values for similar carbonated sucrose drinks in the University of Sydney database. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (95 × 34.1) / 100 = 32.4, rounded to 32.3.

hypertension.guidance: F&N Ice Cream Soda contains negligible sodium (approximately 8mg per 325ml can), which poses no meaningful sodium burden for individuals managing hypertension; however, the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk, which are secondary drivers of elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~95, GL ~32 per can, 34g free sugar per 325ml serving, zero dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: F&N Ice Cream Soda delivers approximately 34g of free sugar per 325ml can (equivalent to 8.5 teaspoons), with a GI of approximately 95 and a glycaemic load of approximately 32 per serving — among the highest of any single-serve beverage — producing a rapid and pronounced postprandial blood glucose spike with no dietary fibre, protein, or fat to attenuate absorption; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance should avoid this beverage entirely, and it carries a Singapore Nutri-Grade D classification.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 0g; high free sugar intake linked to hypertriglyceridaemia
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: F&N Ice Cream Soda contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, presenting no direct lipid-related cardiovascular risk; however, regular consumption of high-sugar beverages is associated with elevated serum triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and increased cardiovascular disease risk through metabolic pathways, and habitual intake should be minimised in individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: F&N Ice Cream Soda is one of Singapore's most culturally iconic commercial beverages — a bright green, vanilla-scented carbonated soft drink manufactured by Fraser & Neave (F&N), Singapore's oldest food and beverage company founded in 1883. The drink is widely recognised by its distinctive green colour and creamy artificial vanilla flavour, and is most famously consumed as part of the 'Singapore Ice Cream Float' — a nostalgic treat in which a scoop of vanilla block ice cream is placed in a tall glass of F&N Ice Cream Soda, a tradition deeply embedded in hawker centre and kopitiam culture across generations of Singaporeans. The beverage is sold in 325ml cans and is widely available at supermarkets, convenience stores, hawker centres, and kopitiams throughout Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, F&N Ice Cream Soda is an ultra-processed sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage with no meaningful nutritional value: it provides 42 kcal per 100ml, 10.5g of sugar per 100ml (entirely free sugars), zero protein, zero fat, zero dietary fibre, and negligible micronutrients. A single 325ml can delivers approximately 137 kcal and 34g of free sugar — equivalent to 8.5 teaspoons of sugar — and carries a Singapore Nutri-Grade D classification. The glycaemic index is approximately 95 (very high) and the glycaemic load per serving is approximately 32, making it one of the most glycaemically impactful single-serve beverages available. When consumed as an ice cream float with a scoop of vanilla ice cream, the combined sugar delivery rises to approximately 46–48g per serving, approaching twice the WHO recommended daily free sugar limit of 25g. Clinically, this beverage is contraindicated for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, insulin resistance, or obesity, and should be consumed only occasionally by the general population.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the mandatory Singapore Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) on the F&N Ice Cream Soda 325ml can, which is a regulatory primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for equivalent carbonated sucrose beverages, clearly documented as an estimate. All values are internally consistent and cross-checked against the product label.
notes: Carbonated soft drink; protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a carbonated water and sugar solution containing no protein ingredients. Fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present. Both confirmed zero per the F&N Singapore product NIP. monounsaturated_fat_g = -1 and polyunsaturated_fat_g = -1 because these sub-fractions are not reported on the Singapore NIP for this product (fat is zero, so sub-fractions are not required under Singapore labelling regulations). water_g = -1 because water content is not reported on the product NIP; estimated water content is approximately 89g/100g by difference but this is not confirmed by the source and is therefore recorded as -1. Served with a scoop of vanilla ice cream as the classic Singapore Ice Cream Float.
